mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising faster than FEDFUNDS, real policy rates fall and equity discount rates compress. That supports multiple expansion in the next 20 trading days and predicts positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds remained near zero while inflation was elevated in late 2021
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in periods where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points, or if the signal loses sign consistency in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE indicates improving household income expectations and lower recession probability. That broadens risk appetite and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.2% in November 2021 after the pandemic labor recovery
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not produce higher-than-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the average return after a UNRATE decline is not positive across two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly acceleration in RSAFS signals stronger nominal consumption and revenue growth for large-cap firms. That revenue tailwind should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:retail sales were still elevated in late 2021
failure_condition: Reject if RSI-like relative strength from RSAFS expansion does not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% in any 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling UNRATE indicates output is expanding without labor-market stress, which lowers recession odds and supports equities. The mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production was above 100 in October 2021 while unemployment was near 4.2%
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and non-rising UNRATE do not show positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value versus either series alone in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in HOUST after prior weakness signals stronger housing demand, credit creation, and downstream consumption. That broad demand improvement should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 5% from the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts were 1520k in October 2021, above the long-run recessionary troughs
failure_condition: Reject if months with HOUST growth above 5% do not outperform baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative for two straight yearly subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp FEDFUNDS increase after a long zero-rate period tightens financial conditions, raises discount rates, and pressures equity valuations. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if tightening episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average post-trigger return is not below zero in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, payroll growth confirms labor-demand strength and lowers cyclical downside risk. The joint labor improvement should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:payrolls were still recovering and unemployment had fallen to 4.2% in November 2021
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down combination does not outperform standalone PAYEMS or UNRATE signals, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in the majority of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock reopening, rising INDPRO accompanied by expanding PAYEMS indicates supply capacity and labor utilization are healing together. That combination reduces earnings uncertainty and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise month over month
evidence_refs: fact:both industrial production and payrolls were recovering into late 2021
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns above baseline, or if the joint signal underperforms each single-series signal over any 18-month window.
